2 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Where trust, technology, leadership, and human potential converge.Technology has never changed faster.
Trust has never mattered more.
Every week, we hear about another breakthrough in artificial intelligence, another disruption to business, another prediction that an entire industry is about to be transformed. Yet beneath the headlines, something deeper is happening.
The systems we rely on to make decisions are changing.
How we discover information is changing.
How businesses earn trust is changing.
And many of the assumptions we’ve built our industries around are quietly becoming obsolete.
Those are the conversations I want to have.
Welcome to The BIG Idea.
I’m Fred Bean, a technology entrepreneur who has spent more than three decades working at the intersection of hospitality, travel, technology, and digital transformation. Throughout my career, I’ve helped some of the world’s largest travel brands solve problems involving distribution, data, customer experience, and digital infrastructure.
What I’ve learned is that the most important ideas are rarely about technology itself.
They’re about people.
Technology simply exposes how we think.
It reveals which systems deserve to survive and which assumptions need to be challenged.
That’s what this publication is about.
Some weeks we’ll explore artificial intelligence and what it really means for business beyond the hype.
Other weeks we’ll examine hospitality, leadership, entrepreneurship, philanthropy, or the hidden infrastructure that quietly shapes the way organizations operate.
One week might begin with a conversation at a Chamber of Commerce board meeting.
Another may start with a question about hotel distribution, trust in AI, or why some of the most valuable business assets are often hiding in plain sight.
The subjects will change.
The approach won’t.
Every article starts with a simple question:
What are we overlooking?
Sometimes the answer is found in technology.
Sometimes it’s found in leadership.
Sometimes it’s hidden inside a metric we’ve accepted for decades without questioning.
The goal isn’t to provide all the answers.
It’s to ask better questions.
Because better questions build better businesses.
This publication is called The BIG Idea, but the name carries a second meaning.
BIG is also the initials of Bean Intellisphere Group, the technology company I founded. While I’ll occasionally draw from experiences across my companies, this isn’t a corporate blog. It isn’t a product catalog or a marketing channel.
It’s a place to think.
To challenge assumptions.
To connect ideas across industries.
To explore how trust, technology, and human behavior intersect in ways that will shape the future.
If something I write causes you to look at a familiar problem differently, then it’s done its job.
Thank you for joining me at the beginning.
The conversation is just getting started.
